finding: Golden regen both surfaces vs .extraction/baseline: types.ui.ts + types.desktop.ts BYTE-IDENTICAL; openapi.{ui,desktop}.json CANONICALLY-EQUAL. N-1/N-2 are migration/build.rs-only (no route/type change) and N-3 adds code behind the default-OFF `module` feature ziee never enables, so ziee links no new symbol. Working tree restored via git checkout --.

EA-schema fingerprint of the current merged set is IDENTICAL to what the pre-change merged set produces: reconstructed the base merged dir (fkeys in the SDK crate, old blind glob) and `diff -rq` vs current migrations-merged is EMPTY (92 files each). The only delta vs the committed .extraction/baseline/schema.fp is the pre-existing `file_workflow_runs` side-table (workflow module migration 202607144231, already in the branch base — I touched no file-domain code), NOT anything I changed. Notification's 4 FK constraints appear byte-identical in both baseline and current fingerprints.

notification_fkeys.sql relocated byte-identically: sha256 02abd32cb2999dbc89135486f98f920aaceae89db8663566e905792a3418b709 before (sdk crate) and after (ziee module). Same basename/version 202607144180 (in the fkeys range, after all 440xx schema migrations + all 4 referenced tables) → sqlx _sqlx_migrations checksum + version preserved; merged set applies cleanly (cargo check -p ziee provisioned+migrated the build DB, exit 0).

N-1 CORE PROOF: applying ONLY sdk/crates/ziee-notification/migrations to a fresh DB with NO conversations/scheduled_tasks/workflow_runs/users succeeds (exit 0, no 'relation does not exist'). notifications table created with all cols; scheduled_task_id/workflow_run_id/conversation_id nullable UUID, user_id NOT NULL, and FK count = 0. New gate standalone_apply_gate.sh proves all three schema-bound crates apply standalone; reintroducing the leak makes ziee-notification FAIL ('relation public.conversations does not exist') — negative control.

grep -rn 'REFERENCES public\.' sdk/crates/*/migrations/ across ALL schema-bound crates: ziee-auth FKs reference only self-created users/groups/auth_providers (CLEAN, applies standalone); ziee-file has NO FKs (workflow_run_id already side-tabled to ziee's file_workflow_runs in chunk ziee-file — CLEAN, applies standalone); ziee-notification was the ONLY leaker (4 domain FKs) and is now fixed. No other domain-FK leak exists.

Fix does NOT weaken enforcement: (a) ziee's final schema still has all 4 notification FK constraints incl. ON DELETE CASCADE/SET NULL (verified in the reproduced fingerprint) — authorship moved, enforcement identical. (b) The `module` feature widens no privilege: DefaultIdentityResolver validates the JWT, loads the acting user, and 403s inactive accounts (unchanged from the reviewed turnkey.rs impl); AuthModule installs a NoopAuthSyncSink/NoopAuthEventSink (drops events, leaks nothing) and seeds session-settings only once. (c) Making the infra crate domain-agnostic is itself an isolation win — an infra migration can no longer silently bind app-domain tables. (d) ziee never links the feature, so its attack surface is unchanged.

N-3 focused test auth_module_nests_under_api_and_resolver_reaches_non_auth_route: build_api_router([GatedProbe, AuthModule], '/api', pool).finish_api → OpenAPI paths contain /api/auth/* (NOT bare /auth), AND an unauthenticated GET /api/whoami (a NON-auth module route gated by RequirePermissions<DefaultIdentityResolver,(AuthProvidersRead,)>) returns 401 (resolver present, token missing) NOT 500 (missing resolver extension) — proving the whole-app resolver layer reaches routes other than auth's own.

N-3 test auth_module_registered_in_slice_and_runs_last: MODULE_ENTRIES (defined in ziee-framework) contains the 'auth' entry with order == i32::MAX under --features module, proving the #[distributed_slice(MODULE_ENTRIES)] registration in the ziee-auth library crate links into the framework's slice. compose_merged_migrations_from is re-exported from ziee_build_support and consumed by ziee's build.rs (cargo check -p ziee exit 0).

The old compose_merged_migrations(merged, roots) signature is preserved and now delegates to compose_merged_migrations_from(merged, roots, &[]) — byte-identical output for any existing caller (skeleton-server/examples). cargo check --workspace (default features) on the SDK is exit 0, so no SDK caller broke.

cargo check -p ziee = 0 (pre-existing warnings only), cargo check -p ziee-desktop = 0, cd sdk && cargo check --workspace = 0 (default), cargo test -p ziee-auth --features module compiles + 2 new tests pass. The `module` feature adds linkme (optional) + tower/http-body-util (dev only); none reachable without the feature. No new warnings introduced by the changed files.

Desktop side proven equal end-to-end: cargo check -p ziee-desktop exit 0 (the desktop crate's build.rs points its query! macros at the same ziee_build_<key> whose schema now includes the ziee-side notification fkeys), and desktop golden (types.desktop.ts BYTE-IDENTICAL, openapi.desktop.json CANONICALLY-EQUAL). The notification schema/fkeys split is invisible to desktop — same merged set.

Post-fix, sdk/crates/ziee-notification names no app-domain table (grep REFERENCES public. → empty) and its migration closure = its own single schema file (ziee-identity ships none). ziee-build-support names no crate (app supplies dirs). ziee-auth's AuthModule names only SDK-internal symbols and is inert for ziee. Each infra piece is now consumable by an app that has a subset of ziee's schema/wiring.
